service.description: Microsoft Fabric appears across 20 normalized findings in 1 checklist families. 1 lower-confidence families broaden design coverage.
service.whatThisMeans: This service is covered mainly by preview or mixed-confidence guidance. Use it for specialist review with explicit validation.

quality.label: Preview · Medium confidence
quality.summary: Use with architectural judgment. Preview content is valuable, but it should not be treated as equivalent to mature review baselines.

items.text: Configure and use On-prem data gateway or Vnet data gateway to connect to sources either on prem or behind a virtual network
items.description: The data gateway lets you connect your Azure and other data services to Microsoft Fabric and the Power Platform to securely communicate with the data source, execute queries, and transmit results back to the service.

items.text: Configure managed private endpoints to access Azure services
items.description: Managed private endpoints are feature that allows secure and private access to data sources from Fabric Spark workloads. You cannot use starter pool with managed PE

items.text: Configure Microsoft Entra ID conditional access if a user is trying to access your Fabric environment
items.description: When a user authenticates access is determined based on a set of policies that might include IP address, location, and managed devices.

items.text: Configure Private Links to access resources in your own Azure vnet i.e traffic coming in your Fabric environment
items.description: Fabric uses a private IP address from your virtual network. The endpoint allows users in your network to communicate with Fabric over the private IP address using private links.

items.text: Configure trusted workspace access to access storage account behind firewall
items.description: Fabric workspaces with a workspace identity can securely read or write to firewall-enabled Azure Data Lake Storage Gen2 accounts through�trusted workspace access�for OneLake shortcuts.

items.text: Encrypt Data at rest
items.description: In Fabric, all data that is stored in OneLake is encrypted at rest

items.text: Encrypt data in transit
items.description: Data in transit across the public internet between Microsoft services is always encrypted with at least TLS 1.2. Fabric negotiates to TLS 1.3 whenever possible.

items.text: Limit access to the data by defining RLS and OLS on semantic models in Power BI
items.description: Use features like RLS and OLS on Power BI to have more security features on semantic models

items.text: Limit access to the data by defining RLS, CLS and dynamic data masking on Warehouse and SQL analytics endpoints
items.description: Use features like RLS, CLS and Dynamic data masking to enhance your data security requirements on sql workloads.

items.text: Provide RBAC roles on storage account to the managed identity to make a successful connection
items.description: Grant the identity permissions on the storage account

items.text: Restrict providing workspace level role to users instead share an item only to the users
items.description: Not all users need to have access on the entire workspace using roles so instead restrict giving roles on the entire workspace and only share the item to the user using share item feature or managing permission in Fabric

items.text: Use managed vnet option if you have network isolation needs
items.description: Managed virtual networks are virtual networks that are created and managed by Microsoft Fabric for each Fabric workspace. Managed virtual networks provide network isolation for Fabric Spark workloads, meaning that the compute clusters are deployed in a dedicated network and are no longer part of the shared virtual network. It is only supported for spark workload in Fabric.

items.text: Use Microsoft Entra ID as default authentication method
items.description: No need to do anything. Every request to connect to Fabric is authenticated with Microsoft Entra ID, allowing users to safely connect to Fabric from their corporate office, when working at home, or from a remote location.

items.text: Use RBAC on Onelake to provide fine grain access on the data in Tables/Files Onelake
items.description: OneLake RBAC uses role assignments to apply permissions to its members.

items.text: Use workspace identity to authenticate to the services
items.description: A Fabric workspace identity is an automatically managed service principal that can be associated with a Fabric workspace. Workspace identities can be created in the workspace settings of any workspace except My workspaces. A workspace identity is automatically assigned the workspace contributor role and has access to workspace items. Limitation: Write to shortcut destination fails when using workspace identity as the authentication method. Connections with workspace-identity-authentication can only be used in Onelake shortcuts and data pipelines.

items.text: Use Workspace roles to provide access to the users on the data
items.description: Fabric controls data access using workspaces. In workspaces, data appears in the form of Fabric items, and users can't view or use items (data) unless you give them access to the workspace. You can find more information about workspace and item permissions, in Permission model.

items.text: When using shortcuts the user identity of the user should have access on the target location of the shortcut as well
items.description: Take into account the access of the user at both target and source location of the shortcut

items.text: You can add Fabric URLs to your allowlist
items.description: optional

items.text: You can add Power BI URLs to your allowlist
items.description: optional

items.text: You can use Azure service tags to enable connections to and from Microsoft Fabric.
items.description: In Azure, a service tag is a defined group of IP addresses that is automatically managed, as a group, to minimize the complexity of updates or changes to network security rules.
